theme: Tile a grid maximizing matched colored edges
title: Frustrated Wang Tiling: Route the Unavoidable Defects
seed_problem: Wang-tile edge-matching tiling of an R x C grid
mutation: composite: replace the decision problem 'does a perfectly matched Wang tiling exist' with a graded frustration-minimization objective (maximize matched borders) on an instance seeded so no perfect tiling exists; the ideal palette is a periodic phase field with a small set of tiles removed (forced defects) plus poison DECOY tiles that make the obvious scanline greedy cascade -- so the winning strategy is global periodic-field recovery + defect routing, not local edge-matching.
strategies: trivial: alternating matched-domino rows A,B,A,B (reproduces the checker baseline R*floor(C/2))

greedy (trap): scanline placing the locally best-matching tile vs left+top neighbors; at each forced-defect cell it grabs the unique score-2 tile, the poison decoy, desynchronizing the rest of that row and column from the phase field

phase-field insight (strong): recognize tile(x,y) as an increment field, tile the grid periodically as tile(c%P, r%Q), and at each forced-defect cell route the defect by placing the present tile matching the most ideal neighbors -- never the decoy
general solver: local search / belief propagation over the frustrated constraint graph to push forced defects onto the globally cheapest seams
